title: Sequence read and write operations
summary: Read and write sequence records safely while preserving headers, ordering, and downstream compatibility.
test_prompts: Plan a safe sequence-read and write step and explain what must be preserved so downstream tools still accept the output.
Tell me how you would rewrite FASTA or FASTQ records without silently breaking headers or ordering.

title: Sequence format conversion
summary: Convert sequence-related file formats with explicit information-loss and downstream-consumer awareness.
test_prompts: Plan a sequence-format conversion and explain what information could be lost or changed for the downstream pipeline.
Summarize how you would convert sequence files while keeping the target consumer's requirements explicit.

title: Compressed sequence-file handling
summary: Handle compressed sequence files without breaking integrity, ordering, or downstream compatibility.
test_prompts: Explain how you would handle compressed sequence files while preserving integrity and compatibility with downstream tools.
Tell me what checks you would run before and after decompressing or recompressing large sequence files.

title: FASTQ quality inspection
summary: Inspect FASTQ-level quality structure before downstream trimming, alignment, or quantification.
test_prompts: Plan a FASTQ-quality inspection and explain what findings should stop downstream analysis before trimming or alignment.
Summarize the main FASTQ-level issues that indicate corrupted or unusable read files.

title: Paired-end sequence validation
summary: Validate paired-end sequencing files so mate structure, naming, and counts stay consistent before alignment.
test_prompts: Explain how you would validate paired-end read files before alignment and what failures would force you to stop.
Tell me how you would detect broken mate structure or sample naming mismatches in paired FASTQ inputs.

title: Sequence filtering
summary: Filter sequence records reproducibly using explicit biological or technical rules.
test_prompts: Plan a sequence-filtering step and explain how you would record filtering rules so downstream analysis stays reproducible.
Summarize what makes a filtering strategy too aggressive to trust without further review.

title: Sequence statistics
summary: Summarize sequence-file statistics that inform data integrity and downstream analysis planning.
test_prompts: Explain how you would summarize sequence statistics so they are useful for downstream planning rather than just generic file metadata.
Tell me what sequence-level metrics are most helpful before deciding on later processing steps.

title: Sequence batch processing
summary: Process batches of sequence files with deterministic selection rules and reproducible output layout.
test_prompts: Plan a batch-processing step for many sequence files and explain how you would avoid mixing or skipping samples.
Summarize the safeguards needed before applying one sequence-file transformation across a whole directory tree.

title: Sequence file preparation workflow
summary: Coordinate sequence-file inspection, validation, conversion, filtering, and batch-safe preparation before downstream omics analysis.
test_prompts: Outline a sequence-file preparation workflow and show where quality, pairing, filtering, and conversion checks should stop downstream analysis.
Adapt a sequence-preparation workflow for a project that starts from compressed paired-end FASTQ files and ends at an analysis-ready handoff.

title: Ribo-seq preprocessing
summary: Preprocess ribosome-profiling data with explicit read-structure, frame, and annotation-aware assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a Ribo-seq preprocessing stage and explain what periodicity or frame-related checks must pass before downstream interpretation.
Tell me how you would prepare ribosome-profiling data without losing the information needed for translation-aware analysis.

title: Translation-efficiency analysis
summary: Estimate translation efficiency using paired RNA and Ribo measurements with explicit model and comparability assumptions.
test_prompts: Design a translation-efficiency analysis and explain what makes the RNA and Ribo inputs compatible enough for joint interpretation.
Summarize the main confounders that can make translation-efficiency estimates misleading.

title: Ribo-seq ORF detection
summary: Detect translated ORFs from ribosome-profiling data with explicit frame support and annotation-aware caveats.
test_prompts: Plan a Ribo-seq ORF-detection analysis and explain what evidence is needed before reporting a novel translated region.
Tell me how you would separate robust translated ORFs from weak frame-noise artifacts.

title: Ribosome-stalling analysis
summary: Analyze ribosome-stalling or pausing signals with explicit positional, protocol, and artifact-aware context.
test_prompts: Design a ribosome-stalling analysis and explain what protocol artifacts you would rule out before trusting pausing signals.
Summarize how you would interpret site-level stalling without overcalling noisy positional effects.

title: Ribo-seq workflow
summary: Coordinate ribosome-profiling preprocessing, translation-efficiency analysis, ORF discovery, and stalling interpretation.
test_prompts: Outline a Ribo-seq workflow and show where preprocessing, translation-efficiency, ORF, and stalling checkpoints should gate interpretation.
Adapt a Ribo-seq workflow for a project that only needs preprocessing plus translation-efficiency analysis and stops there.

title: Horizontal gene-transfer detection
summary: Detect candidate horizontal gene-transfer events with explicit contamination, phylogenetic, and composition-aware checks.
test_prompts: Plan an HGT-detection analysis and explain what evidence you need before reporting a horizontal transfer candidate.
Tell me how you would separate true transfer candidates from contamination or assembly artifacts.

title: Positive-selection analysis
summary: Test for positive-selection signals with explicit alignment, model, and phylogenetic-context requirements.
test_prompts: Design a positive-selection analysis and explain what alignment and phylogenetic checks must be satisfied before trusting the result.
Summarize the main ways poor alignment or model mismatch can create misleading positive-selection signals.

title: Synteny analysis
summary: Analyze syntenic blocks and genome collinearity with explicit assembly and orthology assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a synteny analysis and explain what assembly quality and orthology checks you need before interpreting rearrangements.
Tell me how you would present syntenic-block results without confusing technical fragmentation with biology.

title: Ancestral-sequence reconstruction
summary: Reconstruct ancestral states or sequences with explicit model, node, and uncertainty handling.
test_prompts: Design an ancestral-reconstruction analysis and explain what phylogenetic and model assumptions must be explicit before interpretation.
Summarize how you would communicate uncertainty in reconstructed ancestral states.

title: Comparative-genomics workflow
summary: Coordinate comparative-genomics analyses across selection, synteny, ancestral reconstruction, and transfer-candidate review.
test_prompts: Outline a comparative-genomics workflow and show where alignment, orthology, and topology checks should gate downstream interpretation.
Adapt a comparative-genomics workflow for a project that only needs synteny and positive-selection analysis.

title: PubMed search and fetch
summary: Search and fetch biomedical literature with explicit query scope, provenance, and evidence-target alignment.
test_prompts: Plan a PubMed search-and-fetch workflow and explain how you would keep the query focused on the biological claim the user actually cares about.
Tell me how you would retrieve biomedical papers without mixing loosely related studies into the evidence set.

title: Biomedical literature synthesis
summary: Synthesize literature findings around a focused biomedical question while preserving disagreement and evidence strength.
test_prompts: Design a literature-synthesis approach and explain how you would keep conflicting evidence visible rather than collapsing it into one story.
Summarize how you would synthesize biomedical papers around a focused omics question without drifting into generic background text.

title: Protocol discovery
summary: Discover wet-lab or computational protocols relevant to a biomedical question while keeping reuse boundaries explicit.
test_prompts: Plan a protocol-discovery search and explain how you would separate reusable method leads from fully validated SOPs.
Tell me how you would look for experimental or computational protocols without overstating their fitness for a specific lab setup.

title: Omics evidence mapping
summary: Map omics findings to literature and knowledge-graph evidence with explicit provenance and confidence boundaries.
test_prompts: Design an omics-evidence-mapping analysis and explain how you would preserve source provenance for each evidence link.
Summarize how you would avoid turning weak associative evidence into strong mechanistic claims in an evidence map.

title: Sequence-database search
summary: Search sequence databases and link identifiers with explicit query scope, hit confidence, and interpretation limits.
test_prompts: Plan a sequence-database search and explain how you would separate strong from weak similarity evidence before reporting hits.
Tell me how you would connect sequence-database results back to the intended biological entity without overclaiming identity.

title: Public-data discovery
summary: Discover public omics datasets and study metadata that can support a biomedical question or reanalysis project.
test_prompts: Plan a public-data discovery task and explain what metadata checks you would make before recommending a dataset for reuse.
Summarize how you would search for omics datasets relevant to a biological question without confusing assay type or cohort context.

title: Biomedical semantic search
summary: Run semantic search over biomedical concepts or evidence while keeping topical drift and provenance visible.
test_prompts: Plan a biomedical semantic-search task and explain how you would keep retrieval anchored to the user's actual hypothesis.
Tell me how you would detect when semantic retrieval is drifting too far away from the intended biomedical concept.

title: Literature-to-hypothesis workflow
summary: Coordinate literature retrieval, protocol discovery, evidence mapping, and public-data discovery into a focused biomedical research briefing.
test_prompts: Outline a literature-to-hypothesis workflow and show how retrieval, synthesis, protocol discovery, and public-data review should gate a final research briefing.
Adapt a literature workflow for a project that only needs evidence synthesis plus public-data discovery and does not require protocol leads.
